command.purpose: Select and coordinate an appropriate Java performance test with reproducible workload, environment, thresholds, and result artifacts.

accepted-inputs.heading: Accepted inputs
accepted-inputs.input: Target application, endpoint, workflow, method, or component

ownership.heading: Owner and skills
ownership.item: Associated skills: `@151-java-performance-jmeter`, `@152-java-performance-gatling`, and existing Maven/JMH guidance

command.tool-selection: | Need | Tool | | --- | --- | | HTTP/API load and performance test | JMeter or Gatling | | Scenario-oriented load model and reports | Gatling | | Isolated JVM method or component microbenchmark | JMH |

workflow.step: Clarify target boundary, objective, thresholds, workload, and environment.
Select JMeter, Gatling, or JMH and record the rationale.
Define warm-up, duration, concurrency, data setup, and result artifacts.
Generate or coordinate the reproducible performance workflow using the selected skill or Maven/JMH guidance.
Report limitations, environment metadata, and whether results are comparable.

safeguards.item: Do not present non-equivalent runs as valid before/after comparisons.
Do not use JMeter or Gatling for isolated JVM microbenchmarks when JMH is the correct boundary.
Do not use JMH for end-to-end load behavior.
Keep workload, environment, and threshold assumptions explicit.
